text:
Every pastor is conscious of the problems involved in what may be called pastoral ministry. Problems which are so great that they serve as a great disincentive to large numbers of biblical pastors. So here we have the challenges to God’s servants which hinder them from the effective performance of their ministry as God’s servants. This dissertation sets out to make a qualitative exegesis of 2 Timothy 1:1-18 through investigating the need for what can be termed a ‘theology of perseverance’ for contemporary pastoral ministry using the challenges Paul made on to Timothy and thus positing perseverance as a major component of pastoral theology.

The study attempts to identify the socio-psychological factors of Timothy’s life and the ecclesiastical situation in Ephesus that influenced his theological crisis. The study shows that endurance is not a personal quality but a mandate of the ministry which Paul explicitly stipulates in his exhortations to Timothy. Through a detailed exegesis of the text of 2 Timothy, the study attempts to identify the characteristics of an effective minister of the Gospel and provides insights into the nature of the divine call and the challenges of the spiritual ministry. The study aims at contributing to both the theoretical and practical aspects of the Christian ministry. Today’s church leaders will be motivated to emulate the enduring principles set out in 2 Timothy.
